channel.title: dotnet/fsharp Pull Requests
channel.description: Pull Requests from https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: PR: Cleanup: redundant union case field patterns - @auduchinok
PR: Cleanup: remove redundant qualifiers - @auduchinok
item.description: Removes redundant qualifiers in expressions, patterns, and types.

item.title: PR: Cleanup: remove redundant 'new' - @auduchinok
item.description: Removes redundant 'new' keyword in expressions.

item.title: PR: Cleanup: remove redundant parens - @auduchinok
item.description: Removes some redundant parens in FCS solution.

item.title: PR: Cleanup: remove unused opens - @auduchinok
item.description: Removes unused opens in FCS solution.

item.title: PR: Restore spell checker dictionary - @auduchinok
item.description: Restores the dictionary from https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/7665 that got lost when FCS solution was removed previously. This dictionary adds various F#-compiler-related terms which helps to reduce number of false positive R# spell checker warnings.

item.title: PR: GetTempFile() - @KevinRansom
item.description:
While investigating CI reliability issues we encountered an issue with Path.GetTempFile()

This API has a maxim 64K temp files and will cause tests to fail in wierd ways when the maximum is reached.

The fix is to build a getTempFileName that does not have these limitations.

The majority of the tests clean up the temp files they use, there are some that don't, fixing those tests is beyond the scope of this investigation.

item.title: PR: Fix test failure in main (due to namespace change) - @vzarytovskii
PR: Return Fsc command line args from CoreCompile - @baronfel
item.description:
This enables programmatic builds to easily retrieve the command line for this build.

While investigating the use of Buildalyzer in proj-info for doing out-of-process builds, I realized that I couldn't ever actually retrieve the FscCommandLineArgs property because it was never set. The inner Fsc invocation of the CoreCompile target does set it as an out property, but these args are not readable/usable because the CoreCompile target doesn't actually return them.

This returns them, following the pattern in the C# targets, and I can confirm that I've been able to use this successfully with locally-patched targets files.

item.title: PR: Use ConcurrentDictionary for ProvidedTypeContext dictionaries - @DedSec256
item.description:
We host type providers out-of-process and have our implementation for ProvidedType, in which concurrent read-write operations can occur in the ProvidedTypeContext.

This PR fixes data races in the ProvidedTypeContext.

item.title: PR: Minor test namespace refactor - @KevinRansom
item.description:
Whilst investigating a CI issue, I noticed that we use getTestDirectory from tests.fs in a bunch of the new test framework tests cases.

This refactor breaks that dependence.

  • moves the FSharp.Tests.Utilities namespace up to FSharp.Tests
  • adds to FSharp.Tests.Utilities.Utilities the getTestDirectory function

item.title: PR: Extend SynExpr.IfThenElse with keyword information. - @nojaf
PR: Update F# Compiler Technical Guide link - @nojaf
PR: Fix ranges of function applications in Release code - @dsyme
item.description:
This will reinstate a trimmed down version of https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11717 which was reverted in https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11792

The original PR contained several improvements to debugging ranges for function applications in release code, but the overall "headline" item of avoiding remarking of inline and Release-mode code didn't work like I'd hoped so that change got reverted (it gave good improvements to breakpoints but degraded typical call stacks).

This reinstates the range fixes in the original PR

item.title: PR: Update base directory probing - @KevinRansom
item.description:
@ingted 's original PR and comments are here: https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11309

This update does makes a few changes to clean up the code somewhat

  1. Rebased on latest main
  2. BinFolderOfDefaultFSharpCompiler falls back to tryCurrentDomain .. here: https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/blob/main/src/fsharp/utils/CompilerLocationUtils.fs#L203 so FSharpEnvironment.BinFolderOfDefaultFSharpCompiler(FSharpEnvironment.tryCurrentDomain()) just means doing it twice
  3. Handle fall back in BinFolderOfDefaultFSharpCompiler

item.title: PR: updating microbuild packages - @brettfo
item.description: Updating to the latest MicroBuild packages. Created as draft so I can examine the build outputs.

item.title: PR: Fix tailcalls in async.fs - @dsyme
item.description:
#11807 showed up a bug in async.fs where tailcalls are not being taken, making the use of the trampoline necessary.

We should take tailcalls in any case to keep stack sizes small, this is the fix to make sure we do

I'll have a think how to test this, and also re-run 11807 to check if tests then pass

item.title: PR: update System.Net.Security package from 4.3.0 to 4.3.1 - @brettfo
PR: Experiment: remove trampoline/hijack protection in async.fs and rely on tailcalls being taken - @dsyme
item.description:
This sits over https://github.com/dsyme/fsharp/tree/fix-tc, use https://github.com/dsyme/fsharp/compare/dsyme:fix-tc...dsyme:nojack?expand=1 to compare

I won't commit this, I'm mostly just curious what CI says

The async implementation historically included trampoline/hijack protection so that, after a certain number of operations running an async, the continuation would be hijacked and execution restarted in the trampoline installed at the base of the callstack

This was needed largely because tailcalls could not be guaranteed, especially in partial trust code for .NET Framework 2.0, when tailcalls were often disabled, or if tailcalls were not taken on implementations of .NET like Silverlight.

For .NET Core, we should not need this, as tailcalls are now much more reliable. So this is an experiment to remove the hijack protection altogether and see what passes and what doesn't.

The trampoline is also used when exceptions happen when running asyncs, so is not removed entirely.

item.title: PR: Cleanup and document async.fs - @dsyme
item.description:
The process of fixing bug #11797 via #11799 made me go back and review async.fs end-to-end.

This does the following:

  1. Very carefully documenting exactly when cancellation and trampoline/hijack checks are currently made

  2. Fix https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/issues/8675, where a cancellation check was happening before a try/finally was being entered, causing resources to be potentially dropped on the floor if cancellation happens

  3. Cleanup the code that does CancellationTokenRegistration (AwaitWaitHandle, AwaitBeginEnd etc.) to be more uniform

item.title: PR: RunImmediate should use RunSynchronously if called on UI thread - @dsyme
item.description:
We added RunImmediate in https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11788 and it gives much, much better debug stacks.

However we hadn't realised that in FSharp.Editor this was sometimes being called on the UI thread - notably for GoToDefinition for external sources. This results in a UI hang for this case. Running an async synchronously on the UI thread is not what we want. So we protect against that.

For this case we rename RunImmediate to RunImmediateExceptOnUI.

(RunImmediateExceptOnUI is now effectively the old F# 4.0 spec for RunSynchronously . This got changed in https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11142 to check for isThreadPoolThread, which made our stacks originating from Roslyn background threads worse.)

item.title: PR: Fix 11797 - @dsyme
item.description:
Fixes the regression #11797

The cause was this PR: https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11142. That change installed a new trampoline on each completed task continuation. No new trampoline should be installed when we detect synchronous completion.

item.title: PR: Remove call to SetLength when copying `ReadOnlyMemory<byte>` to a MemoryMapped file - @TIHan
item.description: @vzarytovskii correctly pointed out that we can't set the length of a memory mapped file. It's already fixed length so it should be safe to not do it.

item.title: PR: Add RequireQualifiedAccess to ParsedHashDirectiveArgument type. - @nojaf
item.description: Otherwise, there is quite the collision with the String type. Good spot by @auduchinok!

item.title: PR: Revert "Improve debugging of retail and inline code by not erasing locals and debug points intra-assembly" - @dsyme
item.description: Reverts dotnet/fsharp#11717

item.title: PR: Fix 11771 - cross-project references for projects using generative type providers - @dsyme
item.description:
This fixes regression #11771

  1. The regression was in the addition of these lines: https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11485/files#diff-015b2a27e0e013656556b5d7e867b1f66a17e145606d4a9abea5eeadd8b7820cR1581

  2. We had had this bug before, fixed here: https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/3236.

  3. An explicit test was added for it however it was disabled three years ago or so and never got re-enabled https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/blame/main/tests/service/MultiProjectAnalysisTests.fs#L905

  • [x] I have tested manually
  • [x] I have re-enabled compiler service testing for projects referencing type providers (pending getting it green)
  • [x] I have enabled the testing for .NET Core too (pending getting it green)

item.title: PR: RFC FS-1108 - Undentation frenzy (formatting changes) - @Happypig375
item.description: Base PR: https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11772

item.title: PR: Use RunImmediate for better debug stacks - @dsyme
item.description:
Addresses internal engineering issue https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/issues/11754

Async.RunSynchronously starts the async on a thread pool thread. There is a suggestion to add Async.RunImmediate that never does this. https://github.com/fsharp/fslang-suggestions/issues/1042. This can be defined in terms of Async.StartWithContinuations (which should really be called Async.StartImmediateWithContinuations)

This defines and uses Async.RunImmediate internally in our compiler/service implementation and testing since it gives much better synchronous stacks.

item.title: PR: Correct body SynExpr of parsedData of Lambda when wildcards are involved. - @nojaf
item.description: This is related to https://github.com/fsprojects/fantomas/issues/1806. In Fantomas, we want to use parsedData of SynExpr.Lambda to print out the body expression of the lambda. This is currently wrapped in match expressions when wildcards are involved. This PR tries to resolve this.

item.title: PR: [release/dev17.0] deploy shim helpers - @brettfo
item.description: Follow-up to #11705, fixes #11777.

item.title: PR: [Experiment] `FSharpProject` snapshot - @TIHan
item.description:
This is currently an experiment.

  • Adds a new type: FSharpProject
    • The type represents a F# project for use in an IDE/editor and is internally implemented by an incremental builder which contains the state; the state is immutable.
    • The type can be instantiated separately from FSharpChecker.
    • Because the internal state is immutable, any logical update that needs to be done to the project will result in the creation of a new FSharpProject instance; effectively making it a snapshot.
    • This is useful for few reasons:
      • Synchronizing IDE/editor's view of a project with FSharpProject becomes easier to manage as the IDE/editor is in charge of maintaining the state rather than FCS; this is hugely important.
      • Making an update to a FSharpProject will not impact other FSharpProject instances.
      • Being able to reason about the state of a FSharpProject is easier.
    • Open Questions
      • How is type provider invalidation going to be handled? Type providers can inherently be mutable, making the FSharpProject technically not immutable if it references a type provider.
      • Do we have any APIs that rely on forward looking ahead of files in a project?

item.title: PR: RFC FS-1108 - Undentation frenzy - @Happypig375
item.description: RFC draft is here https://github.com/fsharp/fslang-design/pull/603

item.title: PR: second part of #11755 - @dsyme
item.description:
https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/pull/11755 contained two fixes - the second noticed when I ramped up the testing for debug mode code.

However we want to take the minimal fix for the regression, and the second bug fix is not complete

So this is tracking the second bug fix
